Zajęcia 8

Autor: SO

2026-04-16

# Wymagane pakiety ----
library(tm)           # Przetwarzanie tekstu
## Ładowanie wymaganego pakietu: NLP
library(SnowballC)    # Stemming
library(cluster)      # Klastrowanie
library(wordcloud)    # Chmury sĹ‚Ăłw
## Ładowanie wymaganego pakietu: RColorBrewer
library(factoextra)   # Wizualizacje klastrĂłw
## Warning: pakiet 'factoextra' został zbudowany w wersji R 4.5.3
## Ładowanie wymaganego pakietu: ggplot2
## 
## Dołączanie pakietu: 'ggplot2'
## Następujący obiekt został zakryty z 'package:NLP':
## 
##     annotate
## Welcome to factoextra!
## Want to learn more? See two factoextra-related books at https://www.datanovia.com/en/product/practical-guide-to-principal-component-methods-in-r/
library(RColorBrewer) # Kolory
library(ggplot2)      # Wykresy
library(dplyr)        # Przetwarzanie danych
## 
## Dołączanie pakietu: 'dplyr'
## Następujące obiekty zostały zakryte z 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## Następujące obiekty zostały zakryte z 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(ggrepel)      # Dodawania etykiet w wykresach
## Warning: pakiet 'ggrepel' został zbudowany w wersji R 4.5.3
library(DT)           # Interaktywne tabele
## Warning: pakiet 'DT' został zbudowany w wersji R 4.5.3
# Dane tekstowe ----

# Ustaw Working Directory!
# ZaĹ‚aduj dokumenty z folderu
docs <- DirSource("C:/Users/so469349/Desktop/textfolder3")
# W razie potrzeby dostosuj Ĺ›cieĹĽkÄ™
# np.: docs <- DirSource("C:/User/Documents/textfolder")


# UtwĂłrz korpus dokumentĂłw tekstowych
corpus <- VCorpus(docs)


### Gdy tekst znajduje siÄ™ w jednym pliku csv:
### data <- read.csv("file.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, encoding = "UTF-8")
### corpus <- VCorpus(VectorSource(data$text))


# Korpus
inspect(corpus)

# Korpus - zawartoĹ›Ä‡ przykĹ‚adowego elementu
corpus[[1]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 27652
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "What we are witnessing today is the Renewal of the American Spirit."                                                      
## [2] "Our allies will find that America is once again ready to lead."                                                           
## [3] "All the nations of the world -- friend or foe -- will find that America is strong, America is proud, and America is free."
corpus[[1]][2]
## $meta
##   author       : character(0)
##   datetimestamp: 2026-04-16 15:47:25.8436229228973
##   description  : character(0)
##   heading      : character(0)
##   id           : Address.txt
##   language     : en
##   origin       : character(0)
# 1. Przetwarzanie i oczyszczanie tekstu ----
# (Text Preprocessing and Text Cleaning)


# Normalizacja i usuniÄ™cie zbÄ™dnych znakĂłw ----


# Zapewnienie kodowania w caĹ‚ym korpusie
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(function(x) iconv(x, to = "UTF-8", sub = "byte")))



# Funkcja do zamiany znakĂłw na spacjÄ™
toSpace <- content_transformer(function (x, pattern) gsub(pattern, " ", x))


# UsuĹ„ zbÄ™dne znaki lub pozostaĹ‚oĹ›ci url, html itp.

# symbol @
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "@")

# symbol @ ze sĹ‚owem (zazw. nazwa uĹĽytkownika)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "@\\w+")

# linia pionowa
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "\\|")

# tabulatory
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "[ \t]{2,}")

# CAĹY adres URL:
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "(s?)(f|ht)tp(s?)://\\S+\\b")

# http i https
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "http\\w*")

# tylko ukoĹ›nik odwrotny (np. po http)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "/")

# pozostaĹ‚oĹ›Ä‡ po re-tweecie
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "(RT|via)((?:\\b\\W*@\\w+)+)")

# inne pozostaĹ‚oĹ›ci
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "www")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "~")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "Ă˘â‚¬â€ś")


# Sprawdzenie
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "What we are witnessing today is the Renewal of the American Spirit."                                                      
## [2] "Our allies will find that America is once again ready to lead."                                                           
## [3] "All the nations of the world -- friend or foe -- will find that America is strong, America is proud, and America is free."
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(tolower))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeNumbers)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, stopwords("english"))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removePunctuation)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)


# Sprawdzenie
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] " witnessing today renewal american spirit"                                    
## [2] " allies will find america ready lead"                                         
## [3] " nations world friend foe will find america strong america proud america free"
# usuniÄ™cie ewt. zbÄ™dnych nazw wĹ‚asnych
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, c("rose", "roses", "kate", "kates", "iris", "tyler", "tylers", "javi", "javis", "reed", "josh", "joshs", "elliot", "elliots", "julian", "julians", "patrick", "patricks", "margot", "margots", "one", "however", "ladybug"))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)

# Sprawdzenie
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] " witnessing today renewal american spirit"                                    
## [2] " allies will find america ready lead"                                         
## [3] " nations world friend foe will find america strong america proud america free"
# Stemming ----

# zachowaj kopiÄ™ korpusu 
# do uĹĽycia jako dictionary w uzupeĹ‚nianiu rdzeni
corpus_copy <- corpus

# wykonaj stemming w korpusie
corpus_stemmed <- tm_map(corpus, stemDocument)


# Sprawdzenie
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] " witnessing today renewal american spirit"                                    
## [2] " allies will find america ready lead"                                         
## [3] " nations world friend foe will find america strong america proud america free"
# Sprawdzenie
corpus_stemmed[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "wit today renew american spirit"                                            
## [2] "alli will find america readi lead"                                          
## [3] "nation world friend foe will find america strong america proud america free"
# UzupeĹ‚nienie rdzeni sĹ‚Ăłw po stemmingu ----

# funkcja pomocnicza: wykonuje stemCompletion linia po linii
complete_stems <- content_transformer(function(x, dict) {
  x <- unlist(strsplit(x, " "))                  # podziel na sĹ‚owa
  x <- stemCompletion(x, dictionary = corpus_copy, type="longest") # uzupeĹ‚nij rdzenie
  paste(x, collapse = " ")                       # poĹ‚Ä…cz z powrotem w tekst
})

# wykonaj stemCompletion do kaĹĽdego dokumentu w korpusie
corpus_completed <- tm_map(corpus_stemmed, complete_stems, dict = corpus_copy)

# usuĹ„ NA
corpus_completed <- tm_map(corpus_completed, toSpace, "NA")
corpus_completed <- tm_map(corpus_completed, stripWhitespace)


# Sprawdzenie
corpus_completed[[1]][[1]][1]

fighting behind usefulness justifying needless courage sharepoint dreaming filled hearts expressing hopefully stir souls confidence turning hopefully dreaming actions nowhere americaâ\200\231s willingness empowered aspirational burdensome fears inspiration futures boundaries failures pastures guidelines vision blinding doubts asking citizens embracing renewal americanism spiritual asking members congressional joining dreaming biggest bold dare things countries asking everyones watching tonight seize momentary believers believers futures believers americaâ\200\231s thanking godâ\200ť blessed godâ\200ť blessed united statements"
# PorĂłwnaj:
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] " witnessing today renewal american spirit"                                    
## [2] " allies will find america ready lead"                                         
## [3] " nations world friend foe will find america strong america proud america free"
corpus_stemmed[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "wit today renew american spirit"                                            
## [2] "alli will find america readi lead"                                          
## [3] "nation world friend foe will find america strong america proud america free"
# Decyzja dotyczÄ…ca korpusu ----
# NaleĹĽy w tym momencie rozwaĹĽyÄ‡, 
# ktĂłry obiekt uĹĽyÄ‡ do dalszej analizy:
#
# - corpus (oryginalny, bez stemmingu)
# - corpus_stemmed (po stemmingu)
# - corpus_completed (uzupeĹ‚nione rdzenie)





# Tokenizacja ----


# Macierze czÄ™stoĹ›ci TDM i DTM ----


# a) Funkcja TermDocumentMatrix() ----
# tokeny = wiersze, dokumenty = kolumny
tdm <- TermDocumentMatrix(corpus_completed)
tdm
## <<TermDocumentMatrix (terms: 3617, documents: 30)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 14410/94100
## Sparsity           : 87%
## Maximal term length: 31
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
inspect(tdm)
## <<TermDocumentMatrix (terms: 3617, documents: 30)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 14410/94100
## Sparsity           : 87%
## Maximal term length: 31
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
## Sample             :
##                     Docs
## Terms                Address.txt Change.txt Chiefs.txt Florida.txt
##   candidates                  10         15         14          19
##   countries                   22          0         14          38
##   governmentapproved          13         14         21          68
##   knowledge                    5         11         20          14
##   manifestations              13          2         24          16
##   peopleâ\200ť                   15         18         32          35
##   systemic                     8          6          1           3
##   usefulness                  22         15         12          21
##   willingness                 57         12         23          33
##   workarounds                 13         25         12          14
##                     Docs
## Terms                Immigration.txt Knowledge.txt Nomination.txt People.txt
##   candidates                      15            35             12         16
##   countries                       54             0             30         27
##   governmentapproved              85             5             27         59
##   knowledge                       21            21              5         29
##   manifestations                  26             2              8          9
##   peopleâ\200ť                       51             2             15         70
##   systemic                        18            21              9          3
##   usefulness                      20            28              9         17
##   willingness                     97             5             86         40
##   workarounds                     17            11             16         14
##                     Docs
## Terms                Reality.txt Uncertainty.txt
##   candidates                   8              13
##   countries                    0               0
##   governmentapproved           3               1
##   knowledge                    0               9
##   manifestations               2               6
##   peopleâ\200ť                   17               6
##   systemic                    35               0
##   usefulness                  17               3
##   willingness                  8               6
##   workarounds                 13               7
tdm_m <- as.matrix(tdm)

tdm_m[1:5, 1:5]
##                    Docs
## Terms               Address.txt Apparition.txt Architecture.txt Big.txt
##   –initially                  0              4                4       0
##   –stafford                   0              0                0       0
##   ‘incontrovertible           0              0                0       0
##   ‘judgement                  0              1                0       0
##   ‘things                     0              0                0       0
##                    Docs
## Terms               Breakfast.txt
##   –initially                    0
##   –stafford                     0
##   ‘incontrovertible             0
##   ‘judgement                    0
##   ‘things                       0
# MoĹĽna zapisaÄ‡ TDM w pliku .csv
# write.csv(tdm_m, file="TDM.csv")


# b) Funkcja DocumentTermMatrix() ----
# dokumenty = wiersze, tokeny = kolumny
dtm <- DocumentTermMatrix(corpus_completed)
dtm
## <<DocumentTermMatrix (documents: 30, terms: 3617)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 14410/94100
## Sparsity           : 87%
## Maximal term length: 31
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
inspect(dtm)
## <<DocumentTermMatrix (documents: 30, terms: 3617)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 14410/94100
## Sparsity           : 87%
## Maximal term length: 31
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
## Sample             :
##                  Terms
## Docs              candidates countries governmentapproved knowledge
##   Address.txt             10        22                 13         5
##   Change.txt              15         0                 14        11
##   Chiefs.txt              14        14                 21        20
##   Florida.txt             19        38                 68        14
##   Immigration.txt         15        54                 85        21
##   Knowledge.txt           35         0                  5        21
##   Nomination.txt          12        30                 27         5
##   People.txt              16        27                 59        29
##   Reality.txt              8         0                  3         0
##   Uncertainty.txt         13         0                  1         9
##                  Terms
## Docs              manifestations peopleâ\200ť systemic usefulness willingness
##   Address.txt                 13        15        8         22          57
##   Change.txt                   2        18        6         15          12
##   Chiefs.txt                  24        32        1         12          23
##   Florida.txt                 16        35        3         21          33
##   Immigration.txt             26        51       18         20          97
##   Knowledge.txt                2         2       21         28           5
##   Nomination.txt               8        15        9          9          86
##   People.txt                   9        70        3         17          40
##   Reality.txt                  2        17       35         17           8
##   Uncertainty.txt              6         6        0          3           6
##                  Terms
## Docs              workarounds
##   Address.txt              13
##   Change.txt               25
##   Chiefs.txt               12
##   Florida.txt              14
##   Immigration.txt          17
##   Knowledge.txt            11
##   Nomination.txt           16
##   People.txt               14
##   Reality.txt              13
##   Uncertainty.txt           7
dtm_m <- as.matrix(dtm)

dtm_m[1:5, 1:5]
##                   Terms
## Docs               –initially –stafford ‘incontrovertible ‘judgement ‘things
##   Address.txt               0         0                 0          0       0
##   Apparition.txt            4         0                 0          1       0
##   Architecture.txt          4         0                 0          0       0
##   Big.txt                   0         0                 0          0       0
##   Breakfast.txt             0         0                 0          0       0
# MoĹĽna zapisaÄ‡ DTM w pliku .csv
# write.csv(dtm_m, file="DTM.csv")



# 2. Zliczanie czÄ™stoĹ›ci sĹ‚Ăłw ----
# (Word Frequency Count)

# MoĹĽna zliczyÄ‡ same czÄ™stoĹ›ci sĹ‚Ăłw w macierzach
# dla TDM i DTM da to identyczny rezultat
v <- sort(rowSums(tdm_m), decreasing = TRUE)
tdm_df <- data.frame(word = names(v), freq = v)
head(tdm_df, 10)
v2 <- sort(colSums(dtm_m), decreasing = TRUE)
dtm_df <- data.frame(word = names(v2), freq = v2)
head(dtm_df, 10)
# 3. Eksploracyjna analiza danych ----
# (Exploratory Data Analysis, EDA)


# Chmura sĹ‚Ăłw (globalna)
wordcloud(words = tdm_df$word, freq = tdm_df$freq, min.freq = 7, 
          colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"))
## Warning in wordcloud(words = tdm_df$word, freq = tdm_df$freq, min.freq = 7, :

# WyĹ›wietl top 10
print(head(tdm_df, 10))
##                                  word freq
## willingness               willingness  482
## governmentapproved governmentapproved  396
## peopleâ\200ť                   peopleâ\200ť  342
## candidates                 candidates  253
## usefulness                 usefulness  251
## workarounds               workarounds  238
## knowledge                   knowledge  213
## countries                   countries  201
## systemic                     systemic  185
## manifestations         manifestations  176
# 4. InĹĽynieria cech w modelu Bag of Words: ----
# Reprezentacja sĹ‚Ăłw i dokumentĂłw w przestrzeni wektorowej ----
# (Feature Engineering in vector-space BoW model)


# - podejĹ›cie surowych czÄ™stoĹ›ci sĹ‚Ăłw
# (czÄ™stoĹ›Ä‡ sĹ‚owa = liczba wystÄ…pieĹ„ w dokumencie)
# (Raw Word Counts)



# UĹĽyj utworzonej wczeĹ›niej macierzy DTM
dtm
## <<DocumentTermMatrix (documents: 30, terms: 3617)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 14410/94100
## Sparsity           : 87%
## Maximal term length: 31
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
inspect(dtm)
## <<DocumentTermMatrix (documents: 30, terms: 3617)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 14410/94100
## Sparsity           : 87%
## Maximal term length: 31
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
## Sample             :
##                  Terms
## Docs              candidates countries governmentapproved knowledge
##   Address.txt             10        22                 13         5
##   Change.txt              15         0                 14        11
##   Chiefs.txt              14        14                 21        20
##   Florida.txt             19        38                 68        14
##   Immigration.txt         15        54                 85        21
##   Knowledge.txt           35         0                  5        21
##   Nomination.txt          12        30                 27         5
##   People.txt              16        27                 59        29
##   Reality.txt              8         0                  3         0
##   Uncertainty.txt         13         0                  1         9
##                  Terms
## Docs              manifestations peopleâ\200ť systemic usefulness willingness
##   Address.txt                 13        15        8         22          57
##   Change.txt                   2        18        6         15          12
##   Chiefs.txt                  24        32        1         12          23
##   Florida.txt                 16        35        3         21          33
##   Immigration.txt             26        51       18         20          97
##   Knowledge.txt                2         2       21         28           5
##   Nomination.txt               8        15        9          9          86
##   People.txt                   9        70        3         17          40
##   Reality.txt                  2        17       35         17           8
##   Uncertainty.txt              6         6        0          3           6
##                  Terms
## Docs              workarounds
##   Address.txt              13
##   Change.txt               25
##   Chiefs.txt               12
##   Florida.txt              14
##   Immigration.txt          17
##   Knowledge.txt            11
##   Nomination.txt           16
##   People.txt               14
##   Reality.txt              13
##   Uncertainty.txt           7
dtm_m[1:5, 1:5]
##                   Terms
## Docs               –initially –stafford ‘incontrovertible ‘judgement ‘things
##   Address.txt               0         0                 0          0       0
##   Apparition.txt            4         0                 0          1       0
##   Architecture.txt          4         0                 0          0       0
##   Big.txt                   0         0                 0          0       0
##   Breakfast.txt             0         0                 0          0       0
# UCZENIE MASZYNOWE NIENADZOROWANE ----
# (Unsupervised Machine Learning)



# Klastrowanie k-Ĺ›rednich (k-means) ----


# DobĂłr liczby klastrĂłw
# Metoda sylwetki (silhouette)
fviz_nbclust(t(dtm_m), kmeans, method = "silhouette") +
  labs(title = "DobĂłr liczby klastrĂłw", subtitle = "Metoda sylwetki")

# Wykonaj klastrowanie kmeans
# (sprawdĹş wyniki dla k = 3,4,5)
set.seed(123) # ziarno losowe dla replikacji wynikĂłw



# a) Ustaw liczbÄ™ klastrĂłw k = 2 ----
k <- 2 # ustaw liczbÄ™ klastrĂłw


klastrowanie <- kmeans(dtm_m, centers = k)


# Wizualizacja klastrĂłw
fviz_cluster(list(data = dtm_m, cluster = klastrowanie$cluster),
             geom = "point",
             main = "Wizualizacja klastrĂłw dokumentĂłw")

# Interaktywna tabela z przypisaniem dokumentĂłw i top 5 sĹ‚Ăłw
# Dla kaĹĽdego klastra: liczba dokumentĂłw oraz top 5 sĹ‚Ăłw
cluster_info <- lapply(1:k, function(i) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- sort(colSums(cluster_docs), decreasing = TRUE)
  top_words <- paste(names(word_freq)[1:5], collapse = ", ")
  data.frame(
    Klaster = i,
    Liczba_dokumentĂłw = length(cluster_docs_idx),
   top5slĂłw = top_words,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

# PoĹ‚Ä…cz wszystko w ramkÄ™ danych
cluster_info_df <- do.call(rbind, cluster_info)

# Nazwy dokumentĂłw z korpusu
document_names <- names(corpus)

# Tabela przypisania dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw
documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = klastrowanie$cluster,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# DoĹ‚Ä…czamy dane z podsumowania (JOIN po klastrze)
documents_clusters_z_info <- left_join(documents_clusters, cluster_info_df, by = "Klaster")

# Interaktywna tabela z peĹ‚nym podsumowaniem
datatable(documents_clusters_z_info,
          caption = "Dokumenty, klastry, najczÄ™stsze sĹ‚owa i licznoĹ›Ä‡ klastrĂłw",
          rownames = FALSE,
          options = list(pageLength = 10))
# Chmury sĹ‚Ăłw dla kaĹĽdego klastra
for (i in 1:k) {
  # znajdĹş indeksy dokumentĂłw w danym klastrze
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  
  # nazwy plikĂłw odpowiadajÄ…ce dokumentom w tym klastrze
  doc_names <- names(klastrowanie$cluster)[cluster_docs_idx]
  
    # generuj chmurÄ™ sĹ‚Ăłw dla klastra
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- colSums(cluster_docs)
  wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, 
            max.words = 15, colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"))
  title(paste("Chmura sĹ‚Ăłw - Klaster", i))
}
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## governmentapproved could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## –initially could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## knowledge could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## eventuality could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## workarounds could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.

## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## governmentapproved could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.

# a) Przypisanie dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw ----
document_names <- names(corpus)  # Nazwy dokumentĂłw z korpusu
clusters <- klastrowanie$cluster  # Przypisanie dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw

# Ramka danych: dokumenty i ich klastry
documents_clusters <- data.frame(Dokument = document_names,
                                 Klaster = as.factor(clusters))

# PodglÄ…d
print(documents_clusters)
##                          Dokument Klaster
## Address.txt           Address.txt       2
## Apparition.txt     Apparition.txt       1
## Architecture.txt Architecture.txt       1
## Big.txt                   Big.txt       1
## Breakfast.txt       Breakfast.txt       1
## Change.txt             Change.txt       1
## Chiefs.txt             Chiefs.txt       2
## Design.txt             Design.txt       1
## Dilemma.txt           Dilemma.txt       1
## EnterpriseIT.txt EnterpriseIT.txt       1
## Faces.txt               Faces.txt       1
## Failure.txt           Failure.txt       1
## Flexibility.txt   Flexibility.txt       1
## Florida.txt           Florida.txt       2
## ForBI.txt               ForBI.txt       1
## Immigration.txt   Immigration.txt       2
## Knowledge.txt       Knowledge.txt       1
## Managing.txt         Managing.txt       1
## Military.txt         Military.txt       1
## Nomination.txt     Nomination.txt       2
## Objectivity.txt   Objectivity.txt       1
## Parmenides.txt     Parmenides.txt       1
## People.txt             People.txt       2
## Projects.txt         Projects.txt       1
## Reality.txt           Reality.txt       1
## Speech.txt             Speech.txt       1
## Surprise.txt         Surprise.txt       1
## Systems.txt           Systems.txt       1
## Uncertainty.txt   Uncertainty.txt       1
## Within.txt             Within.txt       1
# a) Wizualizacja przypisania dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw ----
ggplot(documents_clusters, aes(x = reorder(Dokument, Klaster), fill = Klaster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "count", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Przypisanie dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw",
       x = "Dokument",
       y = "Liczba wystÄ…pieĹ„ (powinna wynosiÄ‡ 1)",
       fill = "Klaster") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# b) Ustaw liczbÄ™ klastrĂłw k = 3 ----
k <- 3 # ustaw liczbÄ™ klastrĂłw


klastrowanie <- kmeans(dtm_m, centers = k)


# Wizualizacja klastrĂłw
fviz_cluster(list(data = dtm_m, cluster = klastrowanie$cluster),
             geom = "point",
             main = "Wizualizacja klastrĂłw dokumentĂłw")

# Interaktywna tabela z przypisaniem dokumentĂłw i top 5 sĹ‚Ăłw
# Dla kaĹĽdego klastra: liczba dokumentĂłw oraz top 5 sĹ‚Ăłw
cluster_info <- lapply(1:k, function(i) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- sort(colSums(cluster_docs), decreasing = TRUE)
  top_words <- paste(names(word_freq)[1:5], collapse = ", ")
  data.frame(
    Klaster = i,
    Liczba_dokumentĂłw = length(cluster_docs_idx),
   top5slĂłw = top_words,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

# PoĹ‚Ä…cz wszystko w ramkÄ™ danych
cluster_info_df <- do.call(rbind, cluster_info)

# Nazwy dokumentĂłw z korpusu
document_names <- names(corpus)

# Tabela przypisania dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw
documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = klastrowanie$cluster,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# DoĹ‚Ä…czamy dane z podsumowania (JOIN po klastrze)
documents_clusters_z_info <- left_join(documents_clusters, cluster_info_df, by = "Klaster")

# Interaktywna tabela z peĹ‚nym podsumowaniem
datatable(documents_clusters_z_info,
          caption = "Dokumenty, klastry, najczÄ™stsze sĹ‚owa i licznoĹ›Ä‡ klastrĂłw",
          rownames = FALSE,
          options = list(pageLength = 10))
# Chmury sĹ‚Ăłw dla kaĹĽdego klastra
for (i in 1:k) {
  # znajdĹş indeksy dokumentĂłw w danym klastrze
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  
  # nazwy plikĂłw odpowiadajÄ…ce dokumentom w tym klastrze
  doc_names <- names(klastrowanie$cluster)[cluster_docs_idx]
  
  # generuj chmurÄ™ sĹ‚Ăłw dla klastra
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- colSums(cluster_docs)
  wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, 
            max.words = 15, colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"))
  title(paste("Chmura sĹ‚Ăłw - Klaster", i))
}

# Ramka danych: dokumenty i ich klastry

                                 Klaster = as.factor(clusters))

# PodglÄ…d
print(documents_clusters)
##                          Dokument Klaster
## Address.txt           Address.txt       1
## Apparition.txt     Apparition.txt       2
## Architecture.txt Architecture.txt       2
## Big.txt                   Big.txt       2
## Breakfast.txt       Breakfast.txt       2
## Change.txt             Change.txt       2
## Chiefs.txt             Chiefs.txt       1
## Design.txt             Design.txt       2
## Dilemma.txt           Dilemma.txt       2
## EnterpriseIT.txt EnterpriseIT.txt       2

## Flexibility.txt   Flexibility.txt       2

# b) Wizualizacja przypisania dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw ----
ggplot(documents_clusters, aes(x = reorder(Dokument, Klaster), fill = Klaster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "count", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Przypisanie dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw",
       x = "Dokument",
       y = "Liczba wystÄ…pieĹ„ (powinna wynosiÄ‡ 1)",
       fill = "Klaster") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# c) Ustaw liczbÄ™ klastrĂłw k = 4 ----
k <- 4 # ustaw liczbÄ™ klastrĂłw


klastrowanie <- kmeans(dtm_m, centers = k)


# Wizualizacja klastrĂłw
fviz_cluster(list(data = dtm_m, cluster = klastrowanie$cluster),
             geom = "point",
             main = "Wizualizacja klastrĂłw dokumentĂłw")

# Interaktywna tabela z przypisaniem dokumentĂłw i top 5 sĹ‚Ăłw
# Dla kaĹĽdego klastra: liczba dokumentĂłw oraz top 5 sĹ‚Ăłw
cluster_info <- lapply(1:k, function(i) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- sort(colSums(cluster_docs), decreasing = TRUE)
  top_words <- paste(names(word_freq)[1:5], collapse = ", ")
  data.frame(
    Klaster = i,
    Liczba_dokumentĂłw = length(cluster_docs_idx),
   top5slĂłw = top_words,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

# PoĹ‚Ä…cz wszystko w ramkÄ™ danych
cluster_info_df <- do.call(rbind, cluster_info)

# Nazwy dokumentĂłw z korpusu
document_names <- names(corpus)

# Tabela przypisania dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw
documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = klastrowanie$cluster,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# DoĹ‚Ä…czamy dane z podsumowania (JOIN po klastrze)
documents_clusters_z_info <- left_join(documents_clusters, cluster_info_df, by = "Klaster")

# Interaktywna tabela z peĹ‚nym podsumowaniem
datatable(documents_clusters_z_info,
          caption = "Dokumenty, klastry, najczÄ™stsze sĹ‚owa i licznoĹ›Ä‡ klastrĂłw",
          rownames = FALSE,
          options = list(pageLength = 10))
# Chmury sĹ‚Ăłw dla kaĹĽdego klastra
for (i in 1:k) {
  # znajdĹş indeksy dokumentĂłw w danym klastrze
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  
  # nazwy plikĂłw odpowiadajÄ…ce dokumentom w tym klastrze
  doc_names <- names(klastrowanie$cluster)[cluster_docs_idx]
  
  # generuj chmurÄ™ sĹ‚Ăłw dla klastra
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- colSums(cluster_docs)
  wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, 
            max.words = 15, colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"))
  title(paste("Chmura sĹ‚Ăłw - Klaster", i))
}
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## governmentapproved could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.

## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## governmentapproved could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.

## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## problemsolving could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## projectrelated could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## eventuality could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.
## Warning in wordcloud(names(word_freq), freq = word_freq, max.words = 15, :
## managements could not be fit on page. It will not be plotted.

# c) Przypisanie dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw ----
document_names <- names(corpus)  # Nazwy dokumentĂłw z korpusu
clusters <- klastrowanie$cluster  # Przypisanie dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw

# Ramka danych: dokumenty i ich klastry
documents_clusters <- data.frame(Dokument = document_names,
                                 Klaster = as.factor(clusters))

# PodglÄ…d
print(documents_clusters)
##                          Dokument Klaster
## Address.txt           Address.txt       1
## Apparition.txt     Apparition.txt       3
## Architecture.txt Architecture.txt       3
## Big.txt                   Big.txt       3
## Breakfast.txt       Breakfast.txt       2
## Change.txt             Change.txt       2
## Chiefs.txt             Chiefs.txt       2
## Design.txt             Design.txt       3
## Dilemma.txt           Dilemma.txt       3
## EnterpriseIT.txt EnterpriseIT.txt       3
## Faces.txt               Faces.txt       3
## Failure.txt           Failure.txt       2
## Flexibility.txt   Flexibility.txt       3
## Florida.txt           Florida.txt       1
## ForBI.txt               ForBI.txt       3
## Immigration.txt   Immigration.txt       1
## Knowledge.txt       Knowledge.txt       4
## Managing.txt         Managing.txt       3
## Military.txt         Military.txt       2
## Nomination.txt     Nomination.txt       1
## Objectivity.txt   Objectivity.txt       3
## Parmenides.txt     Parmenides.txt       3
## People.txt             People.txt       1
## Projects.txt         Projects.txt       3
## Reality.txt           Reality.txt       3
## Speech.txt             Speech.txt       3
## Surprise.txt         Surprise.txt       3
## Systems.txt           Systems.txt       3
## Uncertainty.txt   Uncertainty.txt       3
## Within.txt             Within.txt       3
# c) Wizualizacja przypisania dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw ----
ggplot(documents_clusters, aes(x = reorder(Dokument, Klaster), fill = Klaster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "count", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Przypisanie dokumentĂłw do klastrĂłw",
       x = "Dokument",
       y = "Liczba wystÄ…pieĹ„ (powinna wynosiÄ‡ 1)",
       fill = "Klaster") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
